title: Attune Template Frontmatter
description: Schema for the YAML frontmatter block of an Attune template. Unknown keys are preserved verbatim (additionalProperties: true) so new fields can be added without breaking older templates.

type.description: Template kind. Drives retrieval ranking and UI affordances. The enum mirrors the directory layout in attune-help/src/attune_help/templates/ — adding a new type here also implies adding the corresponding subdirectory in attune-help.

tags.description: Free-form classification tags. Drawn from corpus vocabulary via autocomplete.

aliases.description: Alternate names this template can be referred to by. Globally unique across the corpus.

summary.description: 1-3 sentence summary used for retrieval boosting.

source.description: Path or label identifying the source-of-truth content that produced this template. Used by the staleness/regen pipeline to detect when a template is out of date. Free-form; conventionally either a repo-relative path (e.g., 'plugin/skills/foo/SKILL.md') or a category (e.g., 'developer-guidance').

confidence.description: Author-asserted trust level for the template's content. 'Verified' means the content was reviewed against its source-of-truth; 'Provisional' means it was generated or drafted but not yet validated. Drives UI affordances (e.g., a badge in the editor and dashboard).

subtype.description: Optional structural sub-classification within a template type. 'procedural' = step-by-step body; 'tabular' = body is primarily a reference table. Used by the editor to pick a body-shape hint and by retrieval to bias result presentation.

category.description: Optional grouping that ties a reference template back to a corpus concept (a Claude Code skill, a developer task, or a tool/MCP). Used by the dashboard to filter the reference index.

hash.description: Semantic hash managed by the staleness/regen pipeline. Read-only in the editor — set by `attune-author regenerate` when re-rendering a template from sources. Manual edits would silently invalidate staleness detection. Some pipelines write `source_hash` instead; see below.

source_hash.description: Canonical name attune-author writes when re-rendering a template from feature source files. Same role as `hash` — the staleness pipeline uses this to detect when the template is out of date. Read-only in the editor.

feature.description: Name of the feature in `<help_dir>/features.yaml` that produced this template. Set by `attune-author generate` / `regenerate`; binds the template back to its source manifest.

depth.description: Which generation depth produced this template (e.g., `concept`, `task`, `reference`). Set by attune-author.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp recorded when attune-author last wrote this template. No `type` constraint because YAML auto-parses ISO datetime literals into native datetime objects, while string literals stay strings — the editor accepts either.

status.description: Lifecycle status. `generated` = fresh from author.generate; `manual` = hand-edited; `stale` = source has drifted; `verified` = reviewed. Managed by the staleness pipeline; do not edit by hand.
